skill.name: lark-slides
skill.description: Lark Slides management skill for creating, reading, and managing PPT presentations via XML format. Use +create for new presentations; XML API for reading full PPT content, creating, and deleting slides.
skill.summary: Create and manage Lark (Feishu) slide presentations using XML format with full CLI support
skill.author: larksuite

security_audit.summary: This is a legitimate Lark Suite CLI documentation skill containing markdown reference files and XML schemas. The static analyzer flagged 1167 potential issues, but after evaluation, ALL high-severity findings are false positives. The scanner detected markdown code fences as shell commands, documentation strings containing terms like 'base64' as weak crypto implementations, and relative paths in markdown links as path traversal. No malicious intent was found. The skill is pure documentation with no executable code.

medium_findings.title: Markdown Code Fences Detected as Shell Commands
medium_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 799 instances of 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' and shell command substitution patterns. These are all FALSE POSITIVES. The backticks are markdown code fences (```bash, ```json, etc.) in documentation files, not actual Ruby shell commands. These are legitimate CLI usage examples in markdown documentation.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: All flagged patterns are markdown code fences in documentation. The SKILL.md and reference files contain only documentation examples, not executable code. No actual shell command execution occurs within the skill files.

medium_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs - Lark API Endpoints
medium_findings.description: 59 instances of hardcoded URLs were detected in documentation files. These are TRUE POSITIVES but LOW severity - they represent legitimate Lark API endpoint documentation. No data exfiltration or unauthorized network communication was found.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: URLs are legitimate Lark API endpoints documented for user reference. No malicious network behavior detected - these are expected in API documentation.

low_findings.title: Path Traversal Sequences in Markdown Links
low_findings.description: 11 instances of path traversal sequences (../) were flagged. All are FALSE POSITIVES - these are relative markdown links and import statements in documentation, not filesystem operations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The '../' patterns appear in markdown links like '../lark-shared/SKILL.md' which are valid relative paths for documentation structure, not filesystem vulnerabilities.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance - CLI Help Commands
low_findings.description: CLI help commands like 'lark-cli slides --help' were flagged as system reconnaissance. These are legitimate documentation patterns showing users how to get help with the CLI.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: These are standard CLI help documentation patterns, not reconnaissance activities. The skill legitimately documents how to use the CLI help system.

dangerous_patterns.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm References
dangerous_patterns.description: 291 instances of 'weak cryptographic algorithm' were flagged. All are FALSE POSITIVES. The scanner detected strings like 'base64', 'SHA', 'MD5' in XSD schema comments and XML attribute names. These are legitimate documentation of Lark's XML schema attributes, not cryptographic implementations.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: These are documentation strings containing technical terms. The XML schema defines legitimate presentation attributes - no actual cryptographic algorithms are implemented or used insecurely.

dangerous_patterns.title: High File Entropy - XSD Schema Files
dangerous_patterns.description: Several XML/XSD files showed high entropy values (6.05-6.82 bits). These are FALSE POSITIVES - the entropy is due to standard XSD schema format with compressed namespace declarations and schema structure, not encrypted or obfuscated content.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Standard XML schema files have inherent structure that causes slightly elevated entropy. The files are legitimate XSD schemas for Lark Slides format, not malware.

content.user_title: Create Lark Slides presentations with XML
content.value_statement: Need to create professional presentations for Lark (Feishu)? This skill provides CLI commands and XML templates to create, read, and manage slide presentations programmatically with full control over formatting, layouts, and content.
actual_capabilities: Create new slide presentations with customizable titles and content
Add, read, and delete individual slides within presentations
Support for rich XML formatting including shapes, text, tables, and charts
Media upload for embedding images in slides
User and bot authentication modes for different access levels
Full XML schema reference for advanced slide customization
limitations: Requires lark-cli to be installed and authenticated
Cannot directly edit individual elements within existing slides - requires full page replacement
Local images must use relative paths within working directory
Complex elements like charts require referencing the full XSD schema

use_cases.title: Generate team meeting presentations
use_cases.description: Quickly create structured meeting agendas with title slides, bullet points, and action items using simple CLI commands.

use_cases.title: Automate weekly status reports
use_cases.description: Build consistent weekly reports with standard templates, charts, and data visualizations for stakeholders.

use_cases.title: Create training materials
use_cases.description: Design professional training slides with formatted text, images, and structured content for employee onboarding.

prompt_templates.title: Simple presentation creation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Lark Slides presentation with the title "{title}" and the following slide content: {slide_descriptions}. Use the lark-slides skill to create it.
prompt_templates.scenario: When user wants to create a basic presentation with one or more slides

prompt_templates.title: Presentation from outline
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a {n}-slide presentation titled "{title}" based on this outline: {outline}. Include proper formatting, visual elements, and ensure consistent styling across all slides.
prompt_templates.scenario: When user provides a detailed outline for a multi-slide presentation

prompt_templates.title: Read and modify existing presentation
prompt_templates.prompt: Read the content of this Lark presentation: {presentation_id}. Then add a new slide with the title "{slide_title}" and content: {content}.
prompt_templates.scenario: When user wants to read an existing presentation and add new content

prompt_templates.title: Advanced custom slides with XML
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a presentation with slides using custom XML elements. Include: {element_descriptions}. Reference the xml-schema-quick-ref.md for proper XML structure.
prompt_templates.scenario: For advanced users who need specific XML formatting

output_examples.input: Create a presentation called 'Q3 Review' with a title slide and one content slide with bullet points
output_examples.output: Created presentation 'Q3 Review' with 2 slides successfully. The presentation is now available in your Lark account and can be opened directly.

output_examples.input: Add an image to an existing presentation
output_examples.output: Image uploaded successfully. Use the returned file_token in your slide XML to reference the image within your presentation.

best_practices: Always use --as user flag by default for personal presentations to ensure proper permissions
Read xml-schema-quick-ref.md before creating slides with custom XML elements
Keep slides concise with one main idea per slide for better audience engagement
anti_patterns: Do not use absolute file paths for local images - always use relative paths from working directory
Avoid creating more than 20 slides in a single +create command - use xml_presentation.slide.create for larger presentations
Do not skip the XML schema reference when working with complex elements like tables or charts

faq.question: What is lark-slides skill?
faq.answer: Lark-slides is a CLI-based skill for creating and managing presentations in Lark (Feishu). It uses XML format to define slide content, providing fine-grained control over presentation elements.

faq.question: Do I need any special software to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, you need lark-cli installed and authenticated. Run 'lark-cli auth login --domain slides' before creating presentations.

faq.question: How do I add images to slides?
faq.answer: For new presentations, reference images with '@./filename.png' in the slide XML. For existing presentations, first upload using 'slides +media-upload' to get a file_token, then use it in your XML.

faq.question: Can I edit individual elements in an existing slide?
faq.answer: No, the XML API requires full page replacement. To modify a slide, read its current XML, make changes, and use xml_presentation.slide.create to replace it.

faq.question: What authentication modes are available?
faq.answer: Use '--as user' (recommended) for personal presentations you own, or '--as bot' when working with bot-created resources or when specifically required.

faq.question: Where can I find the full XML schema?
faq.answer: The complete XSD schema is in references/slides_xml_schema_definition.xml. For quick reference, use references/xml-schema-quick-ref.md which summarizes common elements.
